programmes.show_description: Get-A-Way: Steed finds himself in danger when known spies escape from Oldhill Monastery. Can The Avengers locate the prisoners in time and discover how they were able to escape so freely?
Deal Me Out: Radar hits a local who is famous for jumping in front of vehicles for the compensation. Captain Pak arrives at the 4077th for a marathon poker game disguised as a conference.
Hot Lips and Empty Arms: Hot Lips receives a letter from home causing her to reassess her life; she learns her friend has married a doctor and now has a beautiful home, a pool, and children.
Officers Only: In gratitude for Hawkeye and Trapper saving his son, a general donates an officers' club to the 4077th with negative consequences. Klinger pretends to be pregnant.
Bridge of Sighs: Henry's attention turns to the prospect of love and romance when his ex-girlfriend, who has been living in America for the last thirty years, returns to the UK for a visit.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Man's Best Friend: Part 2: Alex finally meets the family of his dreams but those dreams are soon shattered when he is forced to abandon the dog he has become attached to. (S4 EP2)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Fight For Your Life: A trainer is reminded of his own shady past when a fighter he's been mentoring is asked to take a fall.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

I Was A Middle-Aged Werewolf: On Halloween night, Jonathan helps a little boy get over his fears, and in the process, get even with his older sister!
Jonathan tries to reunite a comedian and his son by getting them to star in a community play. However, the comedian's inability to take anything seriously affects their relationship.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Love: Laura's childhood friend falls in love with Mr Edwards. She doesn't seem to mind the age difference, but Mr Edwards finds it tough.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Alden's Dilemma: Mr Edwards is suspicious of a young, handsome minister sent to Walnut Grove by the diocese, and Reverend Alden feels his position is threatened.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Marvin's Garden: Jenny nearly drowns looking for a locket in the lake. Dr Marvin convinces Laura to let Jenny help with his garden to rehabilitate. She turns out to be his last patient.
The Fox: Grandpa Zeb Walton is just as surely trapped by a set of unique circumstances as a wild, young mountain animal captured by Ben Walton.
The Big Brother: John-Boy undertakes an act of charity that exposes the family to the unexpected wiles of a most unlikely con artist.
The Waiting Game: Lottery fever takes hold in Ballykissangel. Ambrose is sent on an undercover mission that could be the making of him.
Home to Roost: Deborah's mother arrives in Ormston with some surprising news. Meanwhile, Tom has some unusual advice for shopkeeper Mr Boynton.
The Earth's Sweet Being: Dr Napier starts to promote women's health issues. Some of the younger women are receptive, although she gets a frosty reception from some of the older members.
Rumpole and the Miscarriage of Justice: Rumpole agrees to defend a police officer accused of fabricating evidence during a murder trial.
Henry in Love: Family man Henry Blake returns from a trip to Tokyo, much to the relief of Hawkeye and Trapper. Whilst away he has fallen in love with an ex-cheerleader half his age.
For Want of a Boot: Hawkeye's request for boots has not come through. He leans on the supply sergeant who needs dental work and will get boots for Hawkeye if he'll talk to the camp dentist.
Operation Noselift: Private Danny Baker has a big problem and it's attached to his face. Hawkeye calls in Robbins, a plastic surgeon, to help fix the private's nose.
...the Likely Lads?: Stranger on a Train: Bob's in London to buy a wedding present, and Terry's back from Cyprus after a spell in the army. By chance, they meet on the last train home.
...the Likely Lads?: Home Is the Hero: Classic 1970s sitcom starring James Bolam, Rodney Bewes and Brigit Forsyth. Having left the army, Terry discovers that Bob is now engaged to Thelma.
...the Likely Lads?: Cold Feet: Bob is getting ready to marry Thelma, whom he's been courting since form 4B at Park Junior School. Terry's return has caused Bob to have doubts...
The Midas Touch: Steed, Purdey, and Gambit face the terrifying task of preventing the sale of a lethal weapon to the highest bidder.
Have Guns - Will Haggle: Fearing a visiting dignitary is about to head a revolution in his home country, Steed and Tara investigate the theft of three thousand army rifles.
Henry in Love: Family man Henry Blake returns from a trip to Tokyo, much to the relief of Hawkeye and Trapper. Whilst away he has fallen in love with an ex-cheerleader half his age.
For Want of a Boot: Hawkeye's request for boots has not come through. He leans on the supply sergeant who needs dental work and will get boots for Hawkeye if he'll talk to the camp dentist.
Operation Noselift: Private Danny Baker has a big problem and it's attached to his face. Hawkeye calls in Robbins, a plastic surgeon, to help fix the private's nose.
Front Runner: Henry suggests that Matthew should get a job to supplement his college grant. But when Matthew gets a job at his father's firm, will this ultimately help or hinder Henry?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The People Next Door: The people of Mission Estates don't know that one of their own, a doctor, is hiding a secret. Now Jonathan and Mark must help him confront his past.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
I Was A Middle-Aged Werewolf: On Halloween night, Jonathan helps a little boy get over his fears, and in the process, get even with his older sister!

Mark and Jonathan are sent to help a little boy overcome the sudden death of his father. Stevie deals with his grief through his favourite superhero, Amazing Man.
Frank Reilly, an athlete who expects to gain his sight, must counsel another man who's sure to lose his.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Sins Of The Fathers: Sarah Carter's imposing father visits Walnut Grove, demanding they move back to New York. He tries to 'buy' the boys' affection, but Sarah doesn't rise to it.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: The Older Brothers: The Younger Brothers haven't been out of prison very long when they decide to hold Mr Edwards for ransom.

programmes.show_name: New: Little House On The Prairie
programmes.show_description: Once Upon A Time: Laura wins a story-writing contest and is offered the opportunity to publish her writings.
The Test: Olivia Walton must choose between her family and a career when she is offered a remarkable business opportunity.
The Quilting: Mary Ellen stubbornly defies her grandmother when she rebels against a solemn mountain custom. John-Boy then confronts her about her attitude.
Pack up Your Troubles: Padraig receives a visit from his ex-wife and risks losing custody of his son. Meanwhile, the villagers unite as Quigley starts up his new venture.
Blood Relations: Fred Mills is reluctant to start medical treatment and instead concentrates on fulfilling his wish list of activities. Elsewhere, a rash spreads through the village.
A Natural Mistake: A violent physical attack on a homosexual man, a newcomer to Tannochbrae, causes a conflict of loyalties for Dr Finlay. Should friendship come before his responsibilities?
Rumpole and the Eternal Triangle: Rumpole becomes friendly with a concert violinist while trying to clear her husband of murder.
The Chosen People: A Korean family arrives on camp and begins building living quarters. They claim that 4077th is located on the family's farm and they have come to reclaim it.
As You Were: A lull at camp is short lived and casualties start pouring in. Hawkeye operates on Frank's hernia during a power outage. Radar tries to divert mortar fire falling near camp.
Crisis: In the middle of a freezing Korean winter, Henry tells the surgical staff that the enemy has cut their supply lines. Hot Lips suggests that they take drastic emergency measures.
...by Decree: Classic mystery with Christopher Plummer. With the police unable stop Jack the Ripper's reign of terror, Sherlock Holmes descends into underworld of crime to uncover the killer. (1979)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
...by Decree: Classic mystery with Christopher Plummer. With the police unable stop Jack the Ripper's reign of terror, Sherlock Holmes descends into underworld of crime to uncover the killer. (1979)
Cat Amongst The Pigeons: The team need to move quickly to solve the mystery of why a number of ecologists are suddenly being attacked and killed. Is the common link just a coincidence?
